---
title: "Leopold Mareis"
subtitle: "Mathematical Statistician"
image: "images/Mareis_Picture_square.png"
execute: 
  echo: false
  warning: false
about:
  template: trestles
  links:
    - icon: github
      href: https://github.com/lmareis
    - icon: linkedin
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/leopold-mareis-a0a258308
    - text: '<i class="ai ai-orcid fa-lg"></i>'
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2624-6522
    - text: '<i class="ai ai-google-scholar fa-lg"></i>'
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=efey-4sAAAAJ&view_op=list_works&sortby=pubdate
---

```{r setup}
source("_common.R")
```

### About

I am a final year PhD student at the chair of Mathematical Statistics at TUM. I am very fortunate to be supervised by [Mathias Drton](https://www.math.cit.tum.de/math/personen/professuren/drton-mathias/) and funded through [MaRDI](https://www.mardi4nfdi.de/about/mission). 

Before, I was a researcher at the [Fraunhofer Institute for Cognitive Systems IKS](https://www.iks.fraunhofer.de/en.html) in the group for _Reasoned AI Decisions_ under the director [Mario Trapp](https://www.iks.fraunhofer.de/de/institut/institutsleitung/mario-trapp.html).

My research focuses on efficient estimation of causal effects through graphical modeling, with a strong emphasis on practical applicability. My perspective has been shaped by collaborations in medicine, the social sciences, and industry.

[Curriculum Vitae (06/2026)](content/CV_Mareis.pdf)

### Interest

* Graphical Models

* Causal Inference

* Asymptotic Theory

* Experimental Design


### Education

* (expected autumn / 2026) PhD in Mathematical Statistics, Advisor: Mathias Drton, *TU Munich, Germany*

* Master in Mathematics in Operations Research, 2021, *TU Munich, Germany* <br>
Visiting Student, 2019 - 2020, *TU Delft, Netherlands*

* Bachelor in Mathematics, 2018, *TU Munich, Germany*

### Experience 

* Researcher, 2021 – 2024, *Fraunhofer Institute for Cognitive Systems IKS, Munich*
